meta.title: Major oil & gas fields and Arctic energy assets
meta.note: Seed dataset. Locations are approximate basin/field centroids; production figures are approximate latest public values. Verify against operator disclosures before publication.

fields.name: Ghawar
fields.note: World's largest conventional oil field; 48.3 Bbbl proved (2019 disclosure)

fields.name: Safaniya
fields.note: World's largest offshore oil field

fields.name: Khurais
fields.note: One of the largest single increments ever brought online

fields.name: Shaybah
fields.note: Empty Quarter mega-development

fields.name: Greater Burgan
fields.note: Second-largest conventional field; ~66 Bbbl recoverable

fields.name: Rumaila
fields.note: Iraq's largest producing field

fields.name: West Qurna
fields.note: Multi-phase supergiant

fields.name: Kirkuk
fields.note: Historic northern supergiant, 1927 discovery

fields.name: Ahvaz
fields.note: Iran's largest oil field

fields.name: Upper Zakum
fields.note: Among the largest offshore fields globally

fields.name: North Field / South Pars
fields.note: Feeds Qatar's LNG expansion to 142 Mtpa and most of Iran's gas

fields.name: Tengiz
fields.note: Supergiant; future-growth project onstream 2025

fields.name: Kashagan
fields.note: North Caspian; highest-cost supergiant development

fields.name: Samotlor
fields.note: Mature West Siberian supergiant

fields.name: Priobskoye
fields.note: One of Russia's largest producing fields

fields.name: Urengoy
fields.note: Historically the world's second-largest gas field

fields.name: Daqing
fields.note: China's flagship field since 1959

fields.name: Permian Basin
fields.note: World's most productive oil basin

fields.name: Bakken
fields.note: Williston Basin tight oil

fields.name: Eagle Ford
fields.note: South Texas tight oil and condensate

fields.name: Marcellus (Appalachia)
fields.note: Largest US gas-producing region

fields.name: Haynesville
fields.note: LNG-feed gas basin, Gulf Coast adjacent

fields.name: Athabasca Oil Sands
fields.note: Largest single deposit of crude bitumen

fields.name: Cantarell
fields.note: Former 2 Mb/d supergiant in long-term decline

fields.name: Orinoco Belt
fields.note: World's largest heavy-oil accumulation; sanctions-constrained

fields.name: Tupi (Lula)
fields.note: Santos Basin pre-salt anchor

fields.name: Búzios
fields.note: Now the largest deepwater field by output trajectory

fields.name: Vaca Muerta
fields.note: The only large-scale shale success outside North America

fields.name: Stabroek Block
fields.note: Liza, Payara, Yellowtail; fastest supergiant ramp in history

fields.name: Johan Sverdrup
fields.note: Largest North Sea development in decades

fields.name: Troll
fields.note: Anchor of Norwegian gas supply to Europe

fields.name: Hassi Messaoud
fields.note: Algeria's largest oil field

fields.name: Prudhoe Bay
fields.note: Largest North American Arctic oil field; feeds Trans-Alaska Pipeline

fields.name: Willow
fields.note: NPR-A development approved 2023; first oil ~2029

fields.name: Vankor
fields.note: Anchor of Vostok Oil cluster

fields.name: Bovanenkovo
fields.note: Yamal Peninsula's flagship gas field

fields.name: Yamal LNG (Sabetta)
fields.note: Arctic LNG export hub on the Northern Sea Route

fields.name: Arctic LNG 2
fields.note: Gydan Peninsula; development constrained by sanctions

fields.name: Snøhvit
fields.note: Barents Sea gas to Melkøya LNG

fields.name: Goliat
fields.note: First oil field in the Norwegian Barents Sea

fields.name: Johan Castberg
fields.note: Barents Sea; came onstream 2025

fields.name: Disko–Nuussuaq Basin
fields.note: West Greenland frontier; new exploration licensing suspended in 2021

fields.name: Jameson Land Basin
fields.note: East Greenland frontier basin; historically explored, currently inactive
